title: New FW Released With Brilliant UI Format For Indicator and T-Deck

messages.author: skykingisepic
messages.text: There is a new FW contributor and I think you will be impressed with what he as just released for both the Seeed Studio Indicator and the T-Deck/Plus. Both use the same UI. WiFi and BLE supported. https://github.com/dabeani/meshcore (edited)
messages.content_html: There is a new FW contributor and I think you will be impressed with what he as just released for both the Seeed Studio Indicator and the T-Deck/Plus. Both use the same UI. WiFi and BLE supported. https://github.com/dabeani/meshcore (edited)

messages.author: Ed
messages.text: I installed this on a sensecap indicator I've had lying around waiting for MC to be available ... horrible. How are you supposed to type on this display? Unuseable. I reluctantly put it back on MT (not the new UI) so at least it can do something. Can someone PLEASE make a regular firmware that runs on this so I can have it as a MC companion connected via USB as the permanent node for my pc?
messages.content_html: I installed this on a sensecap indicator I've had lying around waiting for MC to be available ... horrible. How are you supposed to type on this display? Unuseable. I reluctantly put it back on MT (not the new UI) so at least it can do something. Can someone PLEASE make a regular firmware that runs on this so I can have it as a MC companion connected via USB as the permanent node for my pc?
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: skykingisepic
messages.text: @Ed Tap on Contact, tap on Msg, tap on Send (or Channels-Public, tap on Send) and you get a very nice popup keyboard. And you don't need a crappy USB it connects via BLE or WiFi which is redundant anyway since it's stand-alone. This is a Beta release so go to the github repo and create an Issue if you find a problem or have a suggestion. Go to the WiKi where I have created a User Guide to help with navigating the UI. Coming here and proclaiming something is 'horrible' without taking the time to learn how to use it is non-productive. (edited)
messages.content_html: @Ed Tap on Contact, tap on Msg, tap on Send (or Channels-Public, tap on Send) and you get a very nice popup keyboard. Coming here and proclaiming something is 'horrible' without taking the time to learn how to use it is non-productive. (edited)

messages.author: skykingisepic
messages.text: First, I am really excited with all of the new innovation focused on MC and especially the stand-alone devices (Indicator and T-Deck). Every new T-Deck UI is closed-source sitting on top of open-source MC After over 40 yrs at this, I know how developers feel about their creations and achieving some sort of recognition for their efforts. I have no issue with developers keeping closed-source while in Beta Test to protect their particular UI and workflow until it matures. Once they have achieved a somewhat finished (it's never finished!) product and made a major public release that's given the public a chance to identify the product with the developer, then it might be appropriate to go open-source. At that point, other developers can fork and publish different flavors of the original which will be a copy-cat but hopefully the public will associate the original UI with the original developer. Credit given where credit is due. So I welcome any and all new projects that expand the MC ecosphere, open or closed. There is even another newcomer to add to the mix: https://forge.hackers.town/Wrewdison/Aurora Try them all, pick your favorite, just realize they're all in Beta and things will change quickly (all for the better we hope) (edited)
messages.content_html: First, I am really excited with all of the new innovation focused on MC and especially the stand-alone devices (Indicator and T-Deck). Every new T-Deck UI is closed-source sitting on top of open-source MC After over 40 yrs at this, I know how developers feel about their creations and achieving some sort of recognition for their efforts. I have no issue with developers keeping closed-source while in Beta Test to protect their particular UI and workflow until it matures. Once they have achieved a somewhat finished (it's never finished!) product and made a major public release that's given the public a chance to identify the product with the developer, then it might be appropriate to go open-source. At that point, other developers can fork and publish different flavors of the original which will be a copy-cat but hopefully the public will associate the original UI with the original developer. Credit given where credit is due. So I welcome any and all new projects that expand the MC ecosphere, open or closed. There is even another newcomer to add to the mix: https://forge.hackers.town/Wrewdison/Aurora Try them all, pick your favorite, just realize they're all in Beta and things will change quickly (all for the better we hope) (edited)

messages.author: jobox
messages.text: I like using this GUI. Unfortunately, however, I cannot configure my repeater (RAK 4631). I tried synchronising the clock, checking the version and other things, but I always got a “timeout” message; even rebooting via the GUI does not work. When I use my T1000e as a bridge, it works.
messages.content_html: I like using this GUI. Unfortunately, however, I cannot configure my repeater (RAK 4631). I tried synchronising the clock, checking the version and other things, but I always got a “timeout” message; even rebooting via the GUI does not work. When I use my T1000e as a bridge, it works.

messages.author: skykingisepic
messages.text: Very nice, well thought out, clean and neat UI. Also check out the User Guide for usage instructions at https://github.com/dabeani/meshcore/wiki/UserGuide Note: because the Indicator has two controllers, you must install the RP2040 firmware (put Indicator into bootloader mode and copy the uf2 file to it's mounted disk) to support sound and the SD Card as well as use the Web Flasher to install the .bin file for the main UI.
messages.content_html: Very nice, well thought out, clean and neat UI. Also check out the User Guide for usage instructions at https://github.com/dabeani/meshcore/wiki/UserGuide Note: because the Indicator has two controllers, you must install the RP2040 firmware (put Indicator into bootloader mode and copy the uf2 file to it's mounted disk) to support sound and the SD Card as well as use the Web Flasher to install the .bin file for the main UI.
messages.reactions_html: 3
